original: Battle at Aquileja: Emperor Theodosius beats emperor Magnus Maximis
Pope Stephen II re-annoints Pippin de Korte as King of France
Battle of Velbuzd: Serbian forces defeat Bulgarian army
Navigator Gil Eanes leaves Cape Bojador for Lisbon
French army recaptures Le Havre after the English garrison surrenders after a siege
Cardinal Granvelle returns to Madrid
English astronomer and mathematician Thomas Harriot introduces potatoes to England on his return from Roanoke Island in modern-day North Carolina
Spanish marshal Piccolomini conquers Schenkenschans
De Croissy succeeds Le Plectia as French Minister of Finance
Monarch Amangkurat II [Sunan Mas] of Mataram surrenders to the VOC (Vereenigde Oost-Indische Compagnie / Dutch East India Company)
Prussian King Frederik Willhelm I orders compulsory education for 5- to 12-year olds
Danish Captain Vitus Bering, on a Russian expedition, is the first European to sight Mount Saint Elias, the second-highest mountain in Canada and the US
Thames River Police is established by an act of British Parliament, replacing the earlier Marine Police (1798-1800) as the world's oldest continuously serving police force
Peru declares independence from Spain (National Day)
Assassination attempt on French King Louis-Philippe in Paris, masterminded by Giuseppe Marco Fieschi, kills 18 people with a 25-barrel volley gun; the king survives
Denmark phases out slavery, beginning with all newborn children of enslaved women; the parent generation is freed the following year
Memnon is the first clipper to reach San Francisco from New York during the Gold Rush, arriving after a voyage of 120 days
First photograph of a total solar eclipse is captured on a daguerreotype by Johann Julius Friedrich Berkowski at the Royal Observatory in Königsberg, Prussia
First use of fingerprints as a means of identification is made by Sir William James Herschel of the Indian Civil Service
French photographer and balloonist Nadar takes the first aerial photo of the French village of Petit-Bicêtre from a balloon 80 meters in the air
Confederate forces defeated at More's Hill, Missouri
Second day of the Battle of Deep Bottom Run, Virginia
The metric system becomes a legal measurement system in the US
Shocks triggered by the volcano Epomeo on the Isle of Ischia, Italy, destroy 1,200 houses in Casamicciola and kill 2,000 people
Vizcaya Bridge, the largest transporter bridge in the world designed by Basque architect Alberto de Palacio, opens over the River Ibaizabal
City of Miami is incorporated
Spanish American War: U.S. Army occupies Ponce and declares victory in Puerto Rico over Spanish forces
Louis Lassing creates the hamburger in Connecticut
Interior Minister of Russia, Vyacheslav Plehve, is assassinated; as leader of the most reactionary elements of government, he is hated for his repressive policies
Rafael Reyes becomes dictator of Colombia after losing Panama
Yankees turn a triple play and defeat Cleveland 6-4
96°F (35.6°C) in De Bilt, Netherlands
Austria-Hungary decides against mediation and declares war on Serbia, the first declaration of war of World War I
Foxtrot is first danced at the New Amsterdam Roof Garden in New York City by Harry Fox
International Federation of Free Trade Unions ("Vrije Vakbewegings Internationale") forms in Amsterdam
US and Panamanian pact about the safeguarding of the Panama Canal
IX Summer Olympic Games open in Amsterdam, Netherlands
Chicago Cardinals become first NFL team to train out of state (Michigan)
114°F (46°C) recorded in Greensburg, Kentucky (state record)
MLB Chicago White Sox score 11 in the 8th to beat the Yankees 14-12 in New York
NFL divides into two five-team divisions
Spain recognizes the USSR
118°F (48°C) recorded Orofino, Idaho (state record)
Eddie Paynter scores 322 for Lancashire against Sussex
Richard Moore scores 316 for Hampshire against Warwickshire
34,000-ton Cunard-White Star liner Mauretania launches in Birkenhead, England
Fifth Dutch government of Prime Minister Hendrikus Colijn falls
Radio Orange begins broadcasting from London
Yankee Charlie Keller hits three home runs to defeat White Sox 10-9
Nazis murder 10,000 Jews in Minsk, Belarus Ghetto
The Zionist partisans in Poland form the Żydowska Organizacja Bojowa (ZOB) or Jewish Fighting Organization
Operation Gomorrah: RAF bombing over Hamburg causes a firestorm that kills 42,600 German civilians
US 8th Army Corps occupies Coutances, France

"Elevator girl" Betty Lou Oliver survives falling 75 stories after fog causes a US bomber plane to crash into the Empire State Building, breaking the cables supporting the elevator she was operating. This remains a world record for the longest survived elevator fall
Japanese premier Suzuki disregards US ultimatum to surrender
US Army B-25 crashes into the 79th floor of the Empire State Building, killing 14
Iuliu Maniu's Farmers' Party is banned in Romania
IG Farben chemical plant explodes in Ludwigshafen, Germany, killing 182
London's Metropolitan Police Flying Squad foils a bullion robbery in the "Battle of London Airport"
The Union Mundial pro Interlingua is founded at the first Interlingua congress in Tours, France
Heavy rain and a mudslide in Isahaya, western Kyūshū, Japan, kill 992
MLB Chicago White Sox outfielder Jim Landis strikes out five times in a loss to the Orioles in Baltimore
Hawaii's first US election sends first Asian Americans to Congress
United Kingdom begins using postal codes
19 die in a train crash in Steelton, Pennsylvania
Mariner I, launched to Venus, falls into the Atlantic Ocean
England is all out for 611 in reply to Australia's 8-656, and the match ends in a draw
Ranger 7 is launched to the Moon and sends back 4,308 TV images
Pirate radio station 390 (Radio Invicta) in England closes down
Dutch end censorship of "Blue Movie"
39th NFL Chicago All-Star Game: Dallas 20, All-Stars 7 (54,162 attendees)
600,000 attend the "Summer Jam" rock festival featuring The Grateful Dead, The Band, and The Allman Brothers Band at Watkins Glen, New York, at the time the largest ever audience at a pop festival
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll in the South Pacific
NASA launches Skylab 3's astronauts (Alan Bean, Owen Garriott, and Jack Lousma) to the space station for a 59-day stay
69 die when packed bus strikes heavy truck in Belem, Brazil
Eldon Joersz and Geo Morgan set a world airspeed record of 3,530 kph
Tangshan Earthquake, 7.6 in magnitude, kills over 300,000 in northern China, the largest loss of life from an earthquake in the 20th century
White Sox pitchers John Odom (5 innings) and Francisco Barrios (4 innings) no-hit the A's
First oil through the Trans-Alaska Pipeline System reaches Valdez, Alaska
Roy Wilkins turns over NAACP leadership to Benjamin Hooks
At Old Timer's Game, it is announced that Martin will again manage the Yankees
Price of gold tops $200 an ounce for the first time
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
France performs a nuclear test
Peru adopts a constitution, and Fernando Belaúnde Terry becomes president
NASA launches Telstar-3A
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
23rd modern Olympic Games open in Los Angeles
Alan Garcia sworn in as President of Peru
Bomb attack in East Beirut kills 25
NASA releases a transcript from the doomed Challenger; pilot Michael Smith can be heard saying, "Uh-oh!" as the spacecraft disintegrates
IBM announces a price hike on older models
Israeli diplomats arrive in Moscow for first visit in 21 years
Jordan cancels $1.3 billion development plan in West Bank
Winnie Mandella's home in Soweto, South Africa, destroyed by arson
Cardinals'Vince Colemanis caught stealing, ending his record streak at 50
NASA's Lewis Research Center in Cleveland announces new high-temperature superconductors able to operate at 33 to 37 gigahertz
Blackout hits Chicago
Buffalo Bills defeat Philadelphia Eagles 17-13 in American Bowl in Wembley
Groundbreaking of Florida Marlins' 7,500-seat spring training stadium
Baseball players decide to strike on August 12, 1994
Last steel beam is placed on Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Museum in Cleveland, Ohio
Texas Rangers pitcher Kenny Rogers throws the only perfect game in franchise history against the California Angels
Norwegian Finance Minister Sigbjorn Johnsen says that Norway should not lower its crude oil production in an attempt to boost world oil prices
Americans Karch Kiraly and Kent Steffes win inaugural beach volleyball gold medal at the Atlanta Olympics; Kiraly is first to win gold in indoor and beach events
Kennewick Man, the remains of a prehistoric man, is discovered near Kennewick, Washington
Guatemala becomes a member of the Berne Convention copyright treaty
Peter Graf, father of Steffi, enters a German jail for tax evasion
Nine coal miners trapped in the flooded Quecreek Mine in Somerset County, Pennsylvania, are rescued after 77 hours underground
A tornado touches down in a residential area in south Birmingham, England, causing £4,000,000 worth of damages and injuring 39 people
The Provisional Irish Republican Army calls an end to its 30-year-long armed campaign in Northern Ireland
Historic Weston-super-Mare Grand Pier burns down for a second time in 80 years
18 people are killed and 11 injured in ethnic clashes in Ethiopia
Ye Shiwen of China sets world record in the women's 400 m individual medley (4:28.43) to take the gold medal at the London Olympics
39 people are killed after a bus veers off a bridge in Avellino Province, Italy
Missiles strike near Gaza's main hospital and a nearby refugee camp on Monday, leaving 10 dead and many wounded; the Israeli Defence Force says they are not responsible
UN Security Council hold emergency meeting calling for an immediate and indefinite humanitarian ceasefire between Israel and Hamas
Earliest evidence of cancer is found in a 1.7 million-year-old toe fossil from Swartkrans Cave, South Africa, published in the South African Journal of Science
Carr Fire reaches Redding, California, with the death toll at 5, having burned 80,000 acres
Longest "blood moon" eclipse of the 21st century, lasting 1 hour and 43 minutes
Evian Championship Women's Golf, Evian Resort GC: South Korean Ko Jin-young wins her second major title of the season by two strokes from Shanshan Feng, Jennifer Kupcho and Kim Hyo-joo
First Fortnite World Cup is won by US teenager Kyle Giersdorf with the largest ever esports prize of $3 million at Arthur Ashe Stadium, New York
Iter, the world's largest nuclear fusion project, begins assembly in Saint-Paul-lez-Durance, France
19-year-old gymnast Daiki Hashimoto of Japan becomes youngest Olympic men's artistic individual all-around champion in history at the Tokyo Games

Japanese swimmer Yui Ohashi wins the women's individual medley double with a victory in the 200 m individual medley at the Tokyo Olympics; 3 days earlier, she won the 400 m individual medley gold medal
Final episodes of TV soap "Neighbours" air in Australia after 37 years
Flooding in Garrett County, eastern Kentucky, kills at least 37 people, with more missing, and puts four million people in Kentucky and surrounding states under flood alert
Opening ceremony for the 2022 Commonwealth Games held in Birmingham, England, featuring giant animatronic bull
Saudi Arabia unveils plans for a futuristic 106-mile one-building city, "The Line," to house 9 million people, powered by renewable energy sources with no roads
Scientists genetically engineer female animals to reproduce without a male for the first time using fruit flies
Typhoon Doksuri makes landfall in China's southeastern Fujian province, causing landslides and flooding, and goes on to flood Beijing, killing at least 11 people
American fencers Lee Kiefer and Lauren Scruggs go 1-2 in the women's foil at the Paris Olympics; Kiefer successfully defends her gold medal from 2020
Cambridge researchers discover a 4,000-year-old Egyptian handprint
India overtakes China as the top exporter of smartphones to the United States, accounting for 44% of US imports as Apple shifts manufacturing to New Delhi due to increased tariff threats
31st predicted perihelion passage of Halley's Comet
Crusaders abandon their siege of Damascus after only four days leading to the disintegration of theSecond Crusade
Maria Theresa of Austria and Prussian KingFrederick the Greatsign a peace treaty in Berlin
Henry James Pye appointed as British Poet Laureate by KingGeorge III
Joséphine de Beauharnais, future Empress of the French and first wife of Napoleon Bonaparte, is released from prison five days after the execution of her first husband,Alexandre de Beauharnais
Mahmud IIsucceeds Mustafa IV as sultan of the Ottoman Empire
First theatrical performance ofMary Shelley's novel "Frankenstein" as "Presumption; or, the Fate of Frankenstein" by Richard Brinsley Peake, starring Thomas Potter Cooke at Covent Garden
Battle of Ezra Chapel: Union forces under Major GeneralWilliam T. Shermandefeat Confederate Lt. General John Bell Hood near Atlanta in Fulton County, Georgia
US Secretary of StateWilliam H. Sewardannounces the ratification of the 14th Amendment by the states, granting citizenship to ex-slaves
British Conservative government led byLord Salisburyforms
A petition organized byKate Sheppard, demanding women's suffrage, is delivered to New Zealand's parliament, signed by over 25,000 women, a fifth of the adult European female population
First Lord of the AdmiraltyWinston Churchillorders British Grand Fleet to Scapa Flow
Due to virtual anarchy, US Marines go ashore in Haiti on orders from PresidentWoodrow Wilson, leading to an occupation that lasts until 1934
Silent Parade organized byJames Weldon Johnson, 10,000 African Americans march on Fifth Avenue in NYC to protest against lynching
Mob hitmanMad Dog Collallegedly participates in a kidnapping attempt that results in the shooting death of a child, which earns him the nickname "Mad Dog"
"White Zombie," the first feature-length zombie film, directed by Victor Halperin and starringBela Lugosi, is released in the US
US PresidentHerbert Hooveruses federal troops to evict the Bonus Army (WWI veterans and their families) from their encampment; four die
First singing telegram delivered to Rudy Vallée in New York City
Italian Fascist dictatorBenito Mussoliniresigns
US PresidentFranklin D. Rooseveltannounces end of coffee rationing in US
Adolf Hitlerreroutes four divisions from southern France to Normandy
Walt Disneyreleases animated musical film "Alice In Wonderland," featuring Kathryn Beaumont, J. Pat O'Malley, Ed Wynn, and Sterling Holloway and songs by Sammy Fain and Bob Hilliard
"On the Waterfront," directed byElia Kazanand starringMarlon Brandoand Eva Marie Saint, is released (Academy Awards Best Picture 1955)
Ernest Bloch's 4th string quartet premieres
Rock and roll piano player-singerJerry Lee Lewismakes his first TV appearance with a raucous rendition of "Whole Lotta Shakin' Goin' On" on "TheSteve AllenShow"
US Republican National convention selectsRichard Nixonas candidate
PresidentLyndon B. Johnsonsends 50,000 more soldiers to Vietnam, bringing the total to 125,000
Sixteen-time Gold GloverBrooks Robinsoncommits three errors in sixth inning
Legendary England cricket all-rounderIan Bothamon debut takes 5 for 74 in the Australian first innings in a seven-wicket third Test win at Trent Bridge
Dave Kingmanbecomes the sixth player to have a second three-home-run game
Lou Brock,Enos Slaughter, A Vaughan, andHoyt Wilhelmare inducted into the Baseball Hall of Fame in Cooperstown, New York
Ángel Cordero Jrbecomes fourth jockey to win 6,000 races
Braves'Dale Murphyhits two three-run home runs in an inning, becoming the 14th man to hit two home runs in an inning and ties the record of six RBIs in an inning
SingerGloria Estefanreleases her debut solo album "Cuts Both Ways"
Alberto Fujimoriinstalled as President of Peru
Dennis Martínezpitches the 13th perfect game in baseball history
R&B singerMary J. Bligereleases her debut album "What's the 411?" with production byPuff Daddy
MarinerKen Griffey Jr.is 3rd to hit HRs in 8 straight games
Art Modell's rep begins secrets talks to move the NFL team Cleveland Browns to Baltimore
NY YankeeDarryl Strawberryhits his 300th home run
NY Ranger centerMark Messiersigns with Vancouver for $20 million over 3 years

Israel criticizesJohn Kerry's proposed ceasefire, stating that no ceasefire deal will be accepted without the destruction of tunnels leading from Gaza to Israel and the demilitarization of the Gaza Strip
Linda Ronstadtreceives the National Medal of Arts from PresidentBarack Obamain a White House ceremony
Pedro Martínez's number 45 is retired by the Boston Red Sox
General John Kelly appointed new Chief of Staff by US PresidentDonald Trump
Pakistan Supreme Court rules corruption accusations against Prime MinisterNawaz Sharifare enough to remove him from office
US Senate vote for "skinny" repeal of the Affordable Care Act (Obamacare) fails 51-49 asJohn McCaincasts the deciding vote against
Meghan, Duchess of Sussex, is revealed as the first guest editor for UK Vogue in 103 years for its September 2019 issue
Former Malaysian Prime MinisterNajib Razakis found guilty of corruption, sentenced to 12 years, and fined nearly $50 million

Australian swimmer Ariarne Titmus claims an Olympic double by winning the women's 200 m freestyle in an Olympic record time of 1:53.50 in Tokyo; 2 days earlier, she defeated American superstarKatie Ledeckyin the 400 m freestyle
French Revolutionary figureMaximilien Robespierreand 22 other leaders of "the Terror" are guillotined to thunderous cheers in Paris

PhysicistRaemer Schreiberand Lieutenant Colonel Peer de Silva arrive on the Pacific island of Tinian with the plutonium core used to assemble the Fat Man bomb used in the bombing of Nagasaki on August 9
